title: uxswitch
text: uxswitch The uxswitch widget is a toggle field control. It always contains a field value, even if it is not a valid Boolean. Unlike the checkbox widget, the uxswitch widget does not provide the indeterminate state, so null value or empty ("") value results in a format error. Default Logical Widgets The default logical widget is ux-Switch . Triggers (web)trigger onchange : This trigger is fired when the uxswitch value is changed. Extends fluent-switch Web Component This widget is implemented using the Fluent web component fluent-switch. Most of its features are also available in this widget. Most of its events are mapped to triggers. For more information, see the Fluent documentation on the fluent-switch web component. Keyboard support When widget has focus, pressing the Spacebar key toggles the widget state. Layout processing Widget class method processLayout() constructs the template HTML for this widget. It generates nodes for the control itself as well as slots for label, checked-message, unchecked-message and error icon, and returns it to Uniface. Use this function to enhance the widget with more static features. Widget properties The uxswitch widget supports the following properties: Table 1. Widget Properties Property Description html:title Defines a hover over text or tooltip, currently used as helper text. html:disabled Defines the widget as being disabled. html:readonly Defines the widget as being read-only. html:hidden Defines a boolean value that hides or shows a switch. html:tabindex Allows or prevents a field from getting sequential focus usually with the Tab key. class:classname Boolean value that adds or removes a specified class. label-text Defines the label text. label-position Defines the position of the label with respect to its widget. The available options are: before (default) after Note: If the label-position is not defined for the widget, it is aligned by default before the widget. unchecked-message Defines a message for an unchecked state. checked-message Defines a message for a checked state. exclude Defines that there will be no column for the field in the uxdatagrid . Note: This is actually not a widget property. It is a field property, used by the uxdatagrid widget if that is the containing entity's widget. Helper and validation error visualization A uxswitch deals with the following error visualizations: Red color border and outline alert Alert icon Tool-tip when hovering the alert icon showing the error message When the widget is in an error state, the checked/unchecked message slot is changed to display the invalid state. By toggling the error is resolved. The error is not brought back by toggling back. UI blocking When the field is in transaction, the uxswitch widget blocks user input by becoming read-only. Widget styling Search: Column CSS Selector Selected node(s) Filter results... 23 rows found CSS Selector Selected node(s) Filter 23 rows found Filter 23 rows found CSS Selector Selected node(s) .u-switch Widget node .u-switch.checked Widget node when the widget is checked .u-switch.u-invalid Widget node when the value is invalid .u-switch.u-format-invalid Widget node when there is format invalid .u-switch.readonly or .u-switch[readonly] Widget node when the widget is read-only .u-switch.disabled or .u-switch[disabled] Widget node when the widget is disabled .u-switch:hover Widget node when the widget is hovered .u-switch:active Widget node when the widget is active .u-switch:focus Widget node when the widget has focus .u-switch:focus-visible Widget node when the widget has focus-visible .u-switch::part(label) Label node .u-switch::part(switch) Switch node .u-switch::part(status-message) Status message node Note: You can apply the order CSS style property to this node to rearrange the position of a status message (both checked and unchecked) with respect to other elements. .u-switch::part(switch-toggle) Switch toggle node .u-switch::part(checked-message) Checked message node .u-switch::part(unchecked-message) Unchecked message node .u-switch::part(error) Error node .u-switch .u-checked-message Checked message text node .u-switch .u-unchecked-message Unchecked message text node .u-switch .u-label-text Label text node .u-switch .u-error-icon Error icon node .u-switch:has(.u-checked-message:not(:empty))::part(status-message) Status message node when a checked message is defined. Note: The reserved width defined for a checked message can be applied to this node. The default width applied is 12ch. .u-switch:has(.u-unchecked-message:not(:empty))::part(status-message) Status message node when an unchecked message is defined. Note: The reserved width defined for an unchecked message can be applied to this node. The default width applied is 12ch. Widget sources The widget source files, such as JavaScript and CSS files, can be downloaded from the following GitHub repository: https://github.com/RocketSoftware/uniface-ux/tree/main/src/ux/switch . Related Links ux-Switch This section provides description for the logical UX widget ux-Switch properties. UX Widgets Uniface UX provides a set of widgets that are implemented using the UX Widget Interface. This set is referred as UX Widgets. The sources of these widgets are available and allow changes and/or extensions to the widgets. In a default Uniface installation, you can find the sources in the <install>\uniface\webapps\uniface\common\ux folder. Was this topic helpful? Like Dislike
